title: ixeris japonica var. litoralis
intro: Ixeris japonica var. litoralis, also known as the Japanese dandelion or seaside dandelion, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to promote digestion and relieve liver and gallbladder issues. Its leaves, roots, and flowers are used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. Ixeris japonica var. litoralis can be consumed as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and its roots can be dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
